profiles.label: Fluid biomarker papers
profiles.definition: Human or other living-organism studies that collect biofluids and evaluate biomarker utility for clinically relevant or pre-clinically informative purposes.
must_address: power and planned versus actual sample size
participant accounting for screened, collected, analyzed, quality-passed, and clinically assessed samples
blinding of biomarker measurement, handling, quality control, and analysis roles
sample timing, collection state, handling conditions, storage, batch assignment, lot bridging, and technical failures
assay validation, replicates, coefficient of variation, lower and upper limits, dilutional linearity, specificity, and spike-recovery
validity and reliability of the clinically relevant assessments paired to the biomarker
statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, effect sizes, confidence intervals, and multiple-comparison management
data availability, analytic code availability, biofluid sample availability, and open/public availability plan

checklist.label: Study registration and analytic plan disclosure
checklist.evidence: The current manuscript pack does not yet name a registry, preregistration record, or pre-specified analytic plan.
checklist.recommendation: Prepare the Transparency, Rigor and Reproducibility Summary to state the actual registration status and whether the analytic plan was pre-specified.

checklist.label: Participant and sample accounting
checklist.evidence: The pack already names endotype context and clinically relevant readouts, but it does not yet enumerate screened, sampled, analyzed, quality-passed, and outcome-assessed participants.
checklist.recommendation: Add a CONSORT-style accounting block for screened, sampled, analyzed, QC-passed, and clinically assessed participants.

checklist.label: Biofluid sample handling, batching, and blinding
checklist.evidence: The pack records sample types and the planned readout lane, but it does not yet capture acquisition timing, handling conditions, batch assignment, freeze-thaw history, or blinded QC roles.
checklist.recommendation: Capture collection timing, subject state, handling/storage conditions, batch assignment, technical failures, and who was blinded during sample handling and quality control.

checklist.label: Assay validation and analytical performance
checklist.evidence: The biomarker panel and readout spine are defined, but assay CV, LLOQ/LLOD, batch reproducibility, dilutional linearity, specificity, and spike-recovery are not yet in the manuscript pack.
checklist.recommendation: Name the assay platform, validation status, replicates, CV, quantitation limits, linearity, specificity, and lot-bridging behavior for the primary biomarker measurements.

checklist.label: Clinically relevant assessment validity
checklist.evidence: The pack ties the biomarker lane to endotypes and downstream burden, but it does not yet document the validity and reliability of the paired clinical assessments.
checklist.recommendation: State which inclusion criteria and outcome measures are established standards, emerging standards, or still provisional, and cite the validation basis.

checklist.label: Statistics, multiple comparisons, and replication
checklist.evidence: No manuscript-facing declaration yet covers statistical assumptions, multiple-comparison handling, or the status of internal/external replication.
checklist.recommendation: Add the planned statistical assumptions, multiple-comparison correction path, outlier/missing-data handling, and the current replication or external-validation status.

checklist.label: Data, code, and sample availability
checklist.evidence: The pack has a source manifest, but it does not yet state repository, DOI, code availability, or future-use permissions for biofluid samples.
checklist.recommendation: Draft the data-availability, analytic-code, and sample-availability statements expected by the JNT biomarker checklist.
